script: Latin romanization — extended Latin alphabet including implosives (ɓ Ɓ, ɗ Ɗ), mid-central vowels (ǝ Ǝ, ɨ Ɨ), special r (ɍ Ɍ). Apostrophe ʼ marks ejectives (k'el, tl'ɨren). No Arabic script; Arabic-script characters in Lagwan body text = error.

paths.notes: LUK 23-24 are withheld from corpus → check-set/kot-check.json (consultant-check sandbox)

borrowings.angel (Arabic ملك): malika — attested throughout NT (not 'anjel' or French 'ange')
borrowings.Messiah / Christ (Arabic): Almassihu — check consistency; may also appear as 'Kristo'
borrowings.holy (Arabic قدس): sɨfaxɨ zi ya — attested phrase for 'holy' in Vushi ha a Sɨfaxɨ zi ya (Holy Spirit)

key_terms_anchor.Holy Spirit: Vushi ha a Sɨfaxɨ zi ya
key_terms_anchor.Christ / Messiah: Masi (attested ×32, written «Masi» in the corpus; e.g. MRK 1:1, 8:29)
key_terms_anchor.temple: mba hɨn msɨnel Malwa (attested Matt 21:13 — confirm standard term)

vowels: a e ǝ i ɨ o u

linter_rules.message: Arabic-script character in Lagwan body text — Lagwan uses Latin only. Remove or transliterate.
French function word in Lagwan body text — likely a slip (proofreading intrusion). Verify it is not part of a Lagwan token or proper name.
Hausa/Nigerian-Arabic form detected — Lagwan has its own attested equivalents. Verify this is not a cross-language leak (Allah → Malwa; mutum → check corpus).
5+ consonants in a row with no vowel — likely a typographical error or encoding issue. Lagwan words have regular V-C-V patterns.
Possible implosive rendering: confirm ɓ/ɗ is used (not plain b/d) where the word requires the implosive. The Settings.xml valid-character set distinguishes b/ɓ and d/ɗ.
Duplicate determiner 'ale ale' — likely a copy-paste or editing artifact.
Lowercase 'malwa' for God — should be capitalized 'Malwa' (proper noun, divine name). Check if intentional.
Non-standard Jesus name: attested form is 'Yasu' in this translation. Standardize.
REVIEW: 'Bɨla ha Dɨma' without 'ale' — check whether the determiner 'ale' is expected in this syntactic position.

density_checks.message: Ratio of 'Malwa' (God) to 'Bɨla ha Dɨma' (Lord) — flag if one is heavily over-represented relative to the source's GOD/LORD distribution (OT passages in particular should distinguish these).

incontext_patterns.name: Sequential narrative — 'Deɗɨ ne'
incontext_patterns.note: 'Deɗɨ ne' = 'Then / At that time' — the standard sequential-narrative connector in Lagwan (check consistency)

incontext_patterns.name: Speech-quote opener — 'a ka … ki'
incontext_patterns.note: Standard Lagwan quotative: SUBJ a ka [ADDRESSEE] ki «…» — all speech events should follow this frame

incontext_patterns.name: Time-setting fronted clause — 'Na zaman/saa/yahɨ'
incontext_patterns.note: Scene-setting fronted temporal: 'Na zaman…', 'Saa hɨn…', 'Na yahɨ…' — discourse boundary marker

incontext_patterns.name: Divine name compound — 'Bɨla ha Dɨma ale'
incontext_patterns.note: Full attested form of 'the LORD' with determiner — used in formal register

structure_patterns.desc: Fronted time clause opening a new scene (Na zaman / Saa hɨn / Na yahɨ / Na ngul)
Lagwan quotative frame: subject + a ka + (addressee) + ki + « ... »
Sequential narrative connector 'Deɗɨ ne' (Then) between scenes
